# Page Title: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu | Leading Engineering Institute for Future Innovators

The Research and Consultancy Section at IIT Jammu is an integral part of the institute. This section works to support research and development activities in the institute, with a goal of enhancing the intellectual property and knowledge base of the institution. It also aims to promote collaboration between the institute and industry, as well as between different departments within the institute.

The Research and Consultancy Section provides a wide range of services to both internal and external clients. These include research projects, industry collaborations, and consulting services. The department often works closely with other departments within the institute, including the various engineering departments, as well as with external partners in industry and academia.

The main functions of Research and Consultancy Section include administrative and accounting support for:

1. Spo

**Checklist – Reimbursement of Travel expenses and Travel Advances**

Following points are to be kept in mind for travel advance and reimbursement process:-

| **Sl. No** | **Checklist Item** | **Description/Notes** | **Mandatory** |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Travel Plan Approval | Prior approval of travel plan (with /without advances) | Yes |  |
| 2 | Travel Allowance Reimbursement (TAD) Form | Form signed by claimant and countersigned by competent authority, if applicable | Yes |  |
| 3 | Filling of Travel Allowance Reimbursement (TAD) Form | Fill complete travel description in TAD form date wise, line wise from place of origin to the end destination & back | Yes |  |
| 4 | Invitation Letter or email / Conference Brochure / Pamphlet (as applicable). | Relevant correspondence to be attached | Yes |  |
| 5

**Q1. Is it mandatory to take prior approval before undertaking travel?**

Ans: Yes. Prior approval is mandatory for every travel visit. The approval must clearly mention the source of funds. The workflow under 'My Activities → Travel Plan' shall be used for obtaining Travel Plan approval. Select Travel Category as Project in case of project related travel.

**Q2. How can I submit the travel reimbursement claim?**

Ans: After obtaining approval of the Travel Plan and completing the journey, the individual must submit the claim for Travel Reimbursement through the same Travel Plan module under which the approval was taken.

**Q3. Which details are mandatory while submitting the reimbursement claim?**

Ans: Complete journey details must be provided, including date-wise/line item-wise travel information along with DA claimed, hotel stay, and other admissible expenses.

**Q4. Can I claim DA and food expenses if the host has already provided boarding and lodging?**

Ans: No. If the host pro

**Q11. Where can I find the detailed travel guidelines and what is the impact of DA enhanced on travel limit?**

Ans: For rates, Notification No.F.No. 3-2/20s06(Admn-I/A&B)/64535 may be referred. For detailed TA rules and guidelines, the document available at the following link should be consulted: https://doe.gov.in/files/circulars_document/TA_Order_dated_23_06_2020_Eng.pdf

**Q 12 Whether self-declaration bills are admissible for travel claim?**

Ans For employees at Pay Level 9 and above, it is mandatory to submit original bills for all admissible expenses (travel tickets, hotel bills, taxi receipts, etc.). Self-declaration in place of bills is not acceptable, except in the case of food/meal bills where actual receipts are not required.

**Q13. Are grocery bills and medicine bills reimbursable through the travel reimbursement module?**

Ans: Ideally the Grocery / Medicine bills are not admissible. However bills related to essential items for field visit may be permissible subject to

### Cdr Avinash Barthwal (Retd)

#### Deputy Registrar

Research and Consultancy

### Message

Research and consultancy are integral parts of IIT Jammu's mission, and we are committed to providing a supportive and efficient environment for research and consultancy activities. We offer a wide range of services such as project management and administrative support. We also ensure that all research activities are conducted in accordance with guidelines and regulatory requirements.

# Page Title: Indian Institute of Technology Jammu | Leading Engineering Institute for Future Innovators

- Frequently Asked Question
- Objectives
- Team
- Documents

The Research and Consultancy Section was setup to provide specialized administrative and managerial support for the operation of Sponsored Research Projects, Consultancy Jobs and other related R&C activities.

1. To ensure that environment is maintained so that faculty are able to conduct quality research.
2. To provide support for researchers to liaise with potential funding sources so as to be able to finance their research and development activities.
3. Providing administrative support for research, management of human resources, financial management.

# Page Title: IIT Jammu RC - Research Cente

### About Us

The Research and Consultancy Section at IIT Jammu is an integral part of the institute. This section works to support research and development activities in the institute, with a goal of enhancing the intellectual property and knowledge base of the institution. It also aims to promote collaboration between the institute and industry, as well as between different departments within the institute.

### Message

Research and consultancy are integral parts of IIT Jammu's mission, and we are committed to providing a supportive and efficient environment for research and consultancy activities. We offer a wide range of services such as project management and administrative support. We also ensure that all research activities are conducted in accordance with guidelines and regulatory requirements.

##### Cdr Avinash Barthwal (Retd)

Deputy Registrar
Research and Consultancy
Cdr Avinash Barthwal is the head or coordinator of the Research Centre (RC) section at IIT Jammu. Designation: Deputy Registrar.
Ashish Dawar is a member of the Research Centre (RC) section at IIT Jammu. Designation: Junior Section Officer. Email: ashish.dawar@iitjammu.ac.in.
Sumit Kumar Sharma is a member of the Research Centre (RC) section at IIT Jammu. Designation: Senior Assistant. Email: sumit.sharma@iitjammu.ac.in.
